{{nihongo|'''Tenmon Missō'''|天文密奏}} refers to the secret reporting to a ruler of observations of abnormal {{ill|astronomical phenomena|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical phenomena|1=astronomical phenomenon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751989}} and their interpretations (predictions) based on {{ill|Astrology|en|Astrology|WD=Q34362|lt=astrology}} in ancient {{ill|East Asia|en|East Asia|WD=Q27231|lt=East Asia}}. It is also known as {{nihongo|'''Tenmon Sō'''|天文奏}}.

== Overview ==
In ancient {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}, the stars in the heavens were thought to symbolize the {{ill|State (polity)|en|State (polity)|WD=Q7275|lt=state}} on Earth, and any anomalies in their movements were perceived as portents of political upheaval ({{ill|Mandate of Heaven|ja|上天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandate of Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} thought). Therefore, it was considered crucial for the ruler to promptly grasp such celestial events as a matter of state governance. Furthermore, it was deemed the duty of the {{ill|Son of Heaven|en|Son of Heaven|WD=Q1147432|lt=Son of Heaven}} to interpret the heavenly will manifest in astronomical phenomena, establish correct calendars and timekeeping, and bestow them upon the people. The {{ill|Book of Jin|en|Book of Jin|WD=Q1154753|lt=Book of Jin}}’s "Astronomical Records," used as a textbook for {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, states that "astronomical anomalies may be omens of fortune, but most are portents of disaster." Thus, it became necessary for those versed in {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=astronomy}} and astrology to report such anomalies and their interpretations to the ruler. This practice was termed "Tenmon Mitsusō."

In Japan, when abnormalities were observed, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (including assistant Tenmon Hakase) or individuals granted a Tenmon Mitsusō {{ill|Senji|ja|宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senji|qq=}} were required to interpret the phenomena based on past cases and submit a sealed report, through the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, to the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}}. The {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code}}’s {{ill|Zatsuryō|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zatsuryō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} specifies: "If there are minor auspicious or inauspicious events, report them to the Onmyōryō. Afterward, seal them seasonally and send them to the Ministry of the Interior to be archived in the national records." This bypassing of the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|WD=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan}}, the highest organ of governance, reflected the emperor's exclusive responsibility for astronomical matters. In later periods, the content of these reports was reviewed and resealed by the Daijōkan or {{ill|Sesshō and Kampaku|ja|摂政・関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sesshō and Kampaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being resubmitted to the Onmyōryō (as described in works such as {{ill|Saigūki|ja|西宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigūki|1=Seikyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627667}}, {{ill|Shingi-shiki|ja|新儀式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingi-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}}). Portions of the reports pertaining to predictions were deemed state secrets and excluded from official records managed by {{ill|Naiki|ja|内記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki|qq=}}.

The documents used for such divinations included the {{ill|Tenmon Yōroku|ja|天文要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Yōroku|1=Tenmon Yōroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442665}}, {{ill|Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Teiran|ja|帝覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiran|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenmonroku|ja|天文録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja|天地瑞祥志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja|指掌宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Senji Ryakketsu|en|Senji Ryakketsu|WD=Q7450796|lt=Senji Ryakketsu}}, and {{ill|Rekirin|ja|暦林|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekirin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

After the establishment of the {{ill|Kurōdodokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōdodokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}}, Tenmon Hakase bypassed the Onmyōryō and submitted reports directly to the {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo}}, who relayed them to the emperor. In urgent cases, such as late at night or early morning, Tenmon Hakase were allowed to report directly to the emperor. The {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}} from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} notes that Tenmon Hakase and qualified secret envoys must immediately report any celestial phenomena to the {{ill|Nairan|en|Nairan|WD=Q5365623|lt=Nairan}}, or regent, without concern for their attire.

Over time, during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, Tenmon Mitsusō privileges became restricted to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}, both of which had deep connections to {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}} of astrology and astronomy. Notably, during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, even the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, associated with calendar-making, were occasionally granted Tenmon Mitsusō rights.
*

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天文密奏]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天文密奏]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天文密奏]]



 


{{nihongo|'''Tenmon Missō'''|天文密奏}} refers to the secret reporting to a ruler of observations of abnormal {{ill|astronomical phenomena|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical phenomena|1=astronomical phenomenon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751989}} and their interpretations (predictions) based on {{ill|Astrology|en|Astrology|WD=Q34362|lt=astrology}} in ancient {{ill|East Asia|en|East Asia|WD=Q27231|lt=East Asia}}. It is also known as {{nihongo|'''Tenmon Sō'''|天文奏}}.

== Overview ==
In ancient {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}, the stars in the heavens were thought to symbolize the {{ill|State (polity)|en|State (polity)|WD=Q7275|lt=state}} on Earth, and any anomalies in their movements were perceived as portents of political upheaval ({{ill|Mandate of Heaven|ja|上天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandate of Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} thought). Therefore, it was considered crucial for the ruler to promptly grasp such celestial events as a matter of state governance. Furthermore, it was deemed the duty of the {{ill|Son of Heaven|en|Son of Heaven|WD=Q1147432|lt=Son of Heaven}} to interpret the heavenly will manifest in astronomical phenomena, establish correct calendars and timekeeping, and bestow them upon the people. The {{ill|Book of Jin|en|Book of Jin|WD=Q1154753|lt=Book of Jin}}’s "Astronomical Records," used as a textbook for {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, states that "astronomical anomalies may be omens of fortune, but most are portents of disaster." Thus, it became necessary for those versed in {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=astronomy}} and astrology to report such anomalies and their interpretations to the ruler. This practice was termed "Tenmon Mitsusō."

In Japan, when abnormalities were observed, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (including assistant Tenmon Hakase) or individuals granted a Tenmon Mitsusō {{ill|Senji|ja|宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senji|qq=}} were required to interpret the phenomena based on past cases and submit a sealed report, through the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, to the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}}. The {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code}}’s {{ill|Zatsuryō|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zatsuryō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} specifies: "If there are minor auspicious or inauspicious events, report them to the Onmyōryō. Afterward, seal them seasonally and send them to the Ministry of the Interior to be archived in the national records." This bypassing of the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|WD=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan}}, the highest organ of governance, reflected the emperor's exclusive responsibility for astronomical matters. In later periods, the content of these reports was reviewed and resealed by the Daijōkan or {{ill|Sesshō and Kampaku|ja|摂政・関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sesshō and Kampaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being resubmitted to the Onmyōryō (as described in works such as {{ill|Saigūki|ja|西宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigūki|1=Seikyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627667}}, {{ill|Shingi-shiki|ja|新儀式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingi-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}}). Portions of the reports pertaining to predictions were deemed state secrets and excluded from official records managed by {{ill|Naiki|ja|内記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki|qq=}}.

The documents used for such divinations included the {{ill|Tenmon Yōroku|ja|天文要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Yōroku|1=Tenmon Yōroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442665}}, {{ill|Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Teiran|ja|帝覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiran|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenmonroku|ja|天文録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja|天地瑞祥志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja|指掌宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Senji Ryakketsu|en|Senji Ryakketsu|WD=Q7450796|lt=Senji Ryakketsu}}, and {{ill|Rekirin|ja|暦林|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekirin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

After the establishment of the {{ill|Kurōdodokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōdodokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}}, Tenmon Hakase bypassed the Onmyōryō and submitted reports directly to the {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo}}, who relayed them to the emperor. In urgent cases, such as late at night or early morning, Tenmon Hakase were allowed to report directly to the emperor. The {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}} from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} notes that Tenmon Hakase and qualified secret envoys must immediately report any celestial phenomena to the {{ill|Nairan|en|Nairan|WD=Q5365623|lt=Nairan}}, or regent, without concern for their attire.

Over time, during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, Tenmon Mitsusō privileges became restricted to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}, both of which had deep connections to {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}} of astrology and astronomy. Notably, during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, even the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, associated with calendar-making, were occasionally granted Tenmon Mitsusō rights.
*

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天文密奏]]



 


{{nihongo|'''Tenmon Missō'''|天文密奏}} refers to the secret reporting to a ruler of observations of abnormal {{ill|astronomical phenomena|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical phenomena|1=astronomical phenomenon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751989}} and their interpretations (predictions) based on {{ill|Astrology|en|Astrology|WD=Q34362|lt=astrology}} in ancient {{ill|East Asia|en|East Asia|WD=Q27231|lt=East Asia}}. It is also known as {{nihongo|'''Tenmon Sō'''|天文奏}}.

== Overview ==
In ancient {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}, the stars in the heavens were thought to symbolize the {{ill|State (polity)|en|State (polity)|WD=Q7275|lt=state}} on Earth, and any anomalies in their movements were perceived as portents of political upheaval ({{ill|Mandate of Heaven|ja|上天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandate of Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} thought). Therefore, it was considered crucial for the ruler to promptly grasp such celestial events as a matter of state governance. Furthermore, it was deemed the duty of the {{ill|Son of Heaven|en|Son of Heaven|WD=Q1147432|lt=Son of Heaven}} to interpret the heavenly will manifest in astronomical phenomena, establish correct calendars and timekeeping, and bestow them upon the people. The {{ill|Book of Jin|en|Book of Jin|WD=Q1154753|lt=Book of Jin}}’s "Astronomical Records," used as a textbook for {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, states that "astronomical anomalies may be omens of fortune, but most are portents of disaster." Thus, it became necessary for those versed in {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=astronomy}} and astrology to report such anomalies and their interpretations to the ruler. This practice was termed "Tenmon Mitsusō."

In Japan, when abnormalities were observed, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (including assistant Tenmon Hakase) or individuals granted a Tenmon Mitsusō {{ill|Senji|ja|宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senji|qq=}} were required to interpret the phenomena based on past cases and submit a sealed report, through the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, to the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}}. The {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code}}’s {{ill|Zatsuryō|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zatsuryō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} specifies: "If there are minor auspicious or inauspicious events, report them to the Onmyōryō. Afterward, seal them seasonally and send them to the Ministry of the Interior to be archived in the national records." This bypassing of the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|WD=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan}}, the highest organ of governance, reflected the emperor's exclusive responsibility for astronomical matters. In later periods, the content of these reports was reviewed and resealed by the Daijōkan or {{ill|Sesshō and Kampaku|ja|摂政・関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sesshō and Kampaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being resubmitted to the Onmyōryō (as described in works such as {{ill|Saigūki|ja|西宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigūki|1=Seikyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627667}}, {{ill|Shingi-shiki|ja|新儀式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingi-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}}). Portions of the reports pertaining to predictions were deemed state secrets and excluded from official records managed by {{ill|Naiki|ja|内記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki|qq=}}.

The documents used for such divinations included the {{ill|Tenmon Yōroku|ja|天文要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Yōroku|1=Tenmon Yōroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442665}}, {{ill|Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Teiran|ja|帝覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiran|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenmonroku|ja|天文録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja|天地瑞祥志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja|指掌宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Senji Ryakketsu|en|Senji Ryakketsu|WD=Q7450796|lt=Senji Ryakketsu}}, and {{ill|Rekirin|ja|暦林|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekirin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

After the establishment of the {{ill|Kurōdodokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōdodokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}}, Tenmon Hakase bypassed the Onmyōryō and submitted reports directly to the {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo}}, who relayed them to the emperor. In urgent cases, such as late at night or early morning, Tenmon Hakase were allowed to report directly to the emperor. The {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}} from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} notes that Tenmon Hakase and qualified secret envoys must immediately report any celestial phenomena to the {{ill|Nairan|en|Nairan|WD=Q5365623|lt=Nairan}}, or regent, without concern for their attire.

Over time, during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, Tenmon Mitsusō privileges became restricted to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}, both of which had deep connections to {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}} of astrology and astronomy. Notably, during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, even the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, associated with calendar-making, were occasionally granted Tenmon Mitsusō rights.
*

{{nihongo|'''Tenmon Missō'''|天文密奏}} refers to the secret reporting to a ruler of observations of abnormal {{ill|astronomical phenomena|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical phenomena|1=Astronomical events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751989}} and their interpretations (predictions) based on {{ill|Astrology|en|Astrology|WD=Q34362|lt=astrology|1=Astrology}} in ancient {{ill|East Asia|en|East Asia|WD=Q27231|lt=East Asia|1=East Asia}}. It is also known as {{nihongo|'''Tenmon Sō'''|天文奏}}.

== Overview ==
In ancient {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China (region)|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}, the stars in the heavens were thought to symbolize the {{ill|State (polity)|en|State (polity)|WD=Q7275|lt=state|1=State (polity)}} on Earth, and any anomalies in their movements were perceived as portents of political upheaval ({{ill|Mandate of Heaven|ja|上天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandate of Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} thought). Therefore, it was considered crucial for the ruler to promptly grasp such celestial events as a matter of state governance. Furthermore, it was deemed the duty of the {{ill|Son of Heaven|en|Son of Heaven|WD=Q1147432|lt=Son of Heaven|1=Son of Heaven}} to interpret the heavenly will manifest in astronomical phenomena, establish correct calendars and timekeeping, and bestow them upon the people. The {{ill|Book of Jin|en|Book of Jin|WD=Q1154753|lt=Book of Jin|1=Book of Jin}}’s "Astronomical Records," used as a textbook for {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, states that "astronomical anomalies may be omens of fortune, but most are portents of disaster." Thus, it became necessary for those versed in {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=astronomy|1=Astronomy}} and astrology to report such anomalies and their interpretations to the ruler. This practice was termed "Tenmon Mitsusō."

In Japan, when abnormalities were observed, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (including assistant Tenmon Hakase) or individuals granted a Tenmon Mitsusō {{ill|Senji|ja|宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senji|qq=}} were required to interpret the phenomena based on past cases and submit a sealed report, through the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, to the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. The {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code|1=Yōrō Code}}’s {{ill|Zatsuryō|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zatsuryō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} specifies: "If there are minor auspicious or inauspicious events, report them to the Onmyōryō. Afterward, seal them seasonally and send them to the Ministry of the Interior to be archived in the national records." This bypassing of the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|WD=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}}, the highest organ of governance, reflected the emperor's exclusive responsibility for astronomical matters. In later periods, the content of these reports was reviewed and resealed by the Daijōkan or {{ill|Sesshō and Kampaku|ja|摂政・関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sesshō and Kampaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being resubmitted to the Onmyōryō (as described in works such as {{ill|Saigūki|ja|西宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigūki|1=Seikyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627667}}, {{ill|Shingi-shiki|ja|新儀式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingi-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}}). Portions of the reports pertaining to predictions were deemed state secrets and excluded from official records managed by {{ill|Naiki|ja|内記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki|qq=}}.

The documents used for such divinations included the {{ill|Tenmon Yōroku|ja|天文要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Yōroku|1=Q11442665|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442665}}, {{ill|Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Teiran|ja|帝覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiran|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenmonroku|ja|天文録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja|天地瑞祥志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja|指掌宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Senji Ryakketsu|en|Senji Ryakketsu|WD=Q7450796|lt=Senji Ryakketsu|1=Senji Ryakketsu}}, and {{ill|Rekirin|ja|暦林|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekirin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

After the establishment of the {{ill|Kurōdodokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōdodokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}}, Tenmon Hakase bypassed the Onmyōryō and submitted reports directly to the {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo|1=Kurōdo-dokoro}}, who relayed them to the emperor. In urgent cases, such as late at night or early morning, Tenmon Hakase were allowed to report directly to the emperor. The {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}} from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} notes that Tenmon Hakase and qualified secret envoys must immediately report any celestial phenomena to the {{ill|Nairan|en|Nairan|WD=Q5365623|lt=Nairan|1=Nairan}}, or regent, without concern for their attire.

Over time, during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, Tenmon Mitsusō privileges became restricted to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}, both of which had deep connections to {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}} of astrology and astronomy. Notably, during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, even the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, associated with calendar-making, were occasionally granted Tenmon Mitsusō rights.
*

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天文密奏]]



 


{{nihongo|'''Tenmon Missō'''|天文密奏}} refers to the secret reporting to a ruler of observations of abnormal {{ill|astronomical phenomena|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical phenomena|1=Astronomical events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751989}} and their interpretations (predictions) based on {{ill|Astrology|en|Astrology|WD=Q34362|lt=astrology|1=Astrology}} in ancient {{ill|East Asia|en|East Asia|WD=Q27231|lt=East Asia|1=East Asia}}. It is also known as {{nihongo|'''Tenmon Sō'''|天文奏}}.

== Overview ==
In ancient {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China (region)|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}, the stars in the heavens were thought to symbolize the {{ill|State (polity)|en|State (polity)|WD=Q7275|lt=state|1=State (polity)}} on Earth, and any anomalies in their movements were perceived as portents of political upheaval ({{ill|Mandate of Heaven|ja|上天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandate of Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} thought). Therefore, it was considered crucial for the ruler to promptly grasp such celestial events as a matter of state governance. Furthermore, it was deemed the duty of the {{ill|Son of Heaven|en|Son of Heaven|WD=Q1147432|lt=Son of Heaven|1=Son of Heaven}} to interpret the heavenly will manifest in astronomical phenomena, establish correct calendars and timekeeping, and bestow them upon the people. The {{ill|Book of Jin|en|Book of Jin|WD=Q1154753|lt=Book of Jin|1=Book of Jin}}’s "Astronomical Records," used as a textbook for {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, states that "astronomical anomalies may be omens of fortune, but most are portents of disaster." Thus, it became necessary for those versed in {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=astronomy|1=Astronomy}} and astrology to report such anomalies and their interpretations to the ruler. This practice was termed "Tenmon Mitsusō."

In Japan, when abnormalities were observed, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (including assistant Tenmon Hakase) or individuals granted a Tenmon Mitsusō {{ill|Senji|ja|宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senji|qq=}} were required to interpret the phenomena based on past cases and submit a sealed report, through the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, to the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. The {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code|1=Yōrō Code}}’s {{ill|Zatsuryō|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zatsuryō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} specifies: "If there are minor auspicious or inauspicious events, report them to the Onmyōryō. Afterward, seal them seasonally and send them to the Ministry of the Interior to be archived in the national records." This bypassing of the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|WD=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}}, the highest organ of governance, reflected the emperor's exclusive responsibility for astronomical matters. In later periods, the content of these reports was reviewed and resealed by the Daijōkan or {{ill|Sesshō and Kampaku|ja|摂政・関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sesshō and Kampaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being resubmitted to the Onmyōryō (as described in works such as {{ill|Saigūki|ja|西宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigūki|1=Seikyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627667}}, {{ill|Shingi-shiki|ja|新儀式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingi-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}}). Portions of the reports pertaining to predictions were deemed state secrets and excluded from official records managed by {{ill|Naiki|ja|内記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki|qq=}}.

The documents used for such divinations included the {{ill|Tenmon Yōroku|ja|天文要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Yōroku|1=Q11442665|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442665}}, {{ill|Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Teiran|ja|帝覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiran|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenmonroku|ja|天文録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja|天地瑞祥志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja|指掌宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Senji Ryakketsu|en|Senji Ryakketsu|WD=Q7450796|lt=Senji Ryakketsu|1=Senji Ryakketsu}}, and {{ill|Rekirin|ja|暦林|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekirin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

After the establishment of the {{ill|Kurōdodokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōdodokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}}, Tenmon Hakase bypassed the Onmyōryō and submitted reports directly to the {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo|1=Kurōdo-dokoro}}, who relayed them to the emperor. In urgent cases, such as late at night or early morning, Tenmon Hakase were allowed to report directly to the emperor. The {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}} from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} notes that Tenmon Hakase and qualified secret envoys must immediately report any celestial phenomena to the {{ill|Nairan|en|Nairan|WD=Q5365623|lt=Nairan|1=Nairan}}, or regent, without concern for their attire.

Over time, during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, Tenmon Mitsusō privileges became restricted to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}, both of which had deep connections to {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}} of astrology and astronomy. Notably, during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, even the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, associated with calendar-making, were occasionally granted Tenmon Mitsusō rights.
*

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天文密奏]]



 


{{nihongo|'''Tenmon Missō'''|天文密奏}} refers to the secret reporting to a ruler of observations of abnormal {{ill|astronomical phenomena|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical phenomena|1=Astronomical events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751989}} and their interpretations (predictions) based on {{ill|Astrology|en|Astrology|WD=Q34362|lt=astrology|1=Astrology}} in ancient {{ill|East Asia|en|East Asia|WD=Q27231|lt=East Asia|1=East Asia}}. It is also known as {{nihongo|'''Tenmon Sō'''|天文奏}}.

== Overview ==
In ancient {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China (region)|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}, the stars in the heavens were thought to symbolize the {{ill|State (polity)|en|State (polity)|WD=Q7275|lt=state|1=State (polity)}} on Earth, and any anomalies in their movements were perceived as portents of political upheaval ({{ill|Mandate of Heaven|ja|上天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandate of Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} thought). Therefore, it was considered crucial for the ruler to promptly grasp such celestial events as a matter of state governance. Furthermore, it was deemed the duty of the {{ill|Son of Heaven|en|Son of Heaven|WD=Q1147432|lt=Son of Heaven|1=Son of Heaven}} to interpret the heavenly will manifest in astronomical phenomena, establish correct calendars and timekeeping, and bestow them upon the people. The {{ill|Book of Jin|en|Book of Jin|WD=Q1154753|lt=Book of Jin|1=Book of Jin}}’s "Astronomical Records," used as a textbook for {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, states that "astronomical anomalies may be omens of fortune, but most are portents of disaster." Thus, it became necessary for those versed in {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=astronomy|1=Astronomy}} and astrology to report such anomalies and their interpretations to the ruler. This practice was termed "Tenmon Mitsusō."

In Japan, when abnormalities were observed, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (including assistant Tenmon Hakase) or individuals granted a Tenmon Mitsusō {{ill|Senji|ja|宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senji|qq=}} were required to interpret the phenomena based on past cases and submit a sealed report, through the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, to the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. The {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code|1=Yōrō Code}}’s {{ill|Zatsuryō|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zatsuryō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} specifies: "If there are minor auspicious or inauspicious events, report them to the Onmyōryō. Afterward, seal them seasonally and send them to the Ministry of the Interior to be archived in the national records." This bypassing of the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|WD=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}}, the highest organ of governance, reflected the emperor's exclusive responsibility for astronomical matters. In later periods, the content of these reports was reviewed and resealed by the Daijōkan or {{ill|Sesshō and Kampaku|ja|摂政・関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sesshō and Kampaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being resubmitted to the Onmyōryō (as described in works such as {{ill|Saigūki|ja|西宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigūki|1=Seikyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627667}}, {{ill|Shingi-shiki|ja|新儀式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingi-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}}). Portions of the reports pertaining to predictions were deemed state secrets and excluded from official records managed by {{ill|Naiki|ja|内記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki|qq=}}.

The documents used for such divinations included the {{ill|Tenmon Yōroku|ja|天文要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Yōroku|1=Q11442665|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442665}}, {{ill|Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Teiran|ja|帝覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiran|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenmonroku|ja|天文録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja|天地瑞祥志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja|指掌宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Senji Ryakketsu|en|Senji Ryakketsu|WD=Q7450796|lt=Senji Ryakketsu|1=Senji Ryakketsu}}, and {{ill|Rekirin|ja|暦林|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekirin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

After the establishment of the {{ill|Kurōdodokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōdodokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}}, Tenmon Hakase bypassed the Onmyōryō and submitted reports directly to the {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo|1=Kurōdo-dokoro}}, who relayed them to the emperor. In urgent cases, such as late at night or early morning, Tenmon Hakase were allowed to report directly to the emperor. The {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}} from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} notes that Tenmon Hakase and qualified secret envoys must immediately report any celestial phenomena to the {{ill|Nairan|en|Nairan|WD=Q5365623|lt=Nairan|1=Nairan}}, or regent, without concern for their attire.

Over time, during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, Tenmon Mitsusō privileges became restricted to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}, both of which had deep connections to {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}} of astrology and astronomy. Notably, during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, even the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, associated with calendar-making, were occasionally granted Tenmon Mitsusō rights.
*

This practice was termed "Tenmon Mitsusō."

In Japan, when abnormalities were observed, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (including assistant Tenmon Hakase) or individuals granted a Tenmon Mitsusō {{ill|Senji|ja|宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senji|qq=}} were required to interpret the phenomena based on past cases and submit a sealed report, through the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, to the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. The {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code|1=Yōrō Code}}’s {{ill|Zatsuryō|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zatsuryō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} specifies: "If there are minor auspicious or inauspicious events, report them to the Onmyōryō. Afterward, seal them seasonally and send them to the Ministry of the Interior to be archived in the national records." This bypassing of the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|WD=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}}, the highest organ of governance, reflected the emperor's exclusive responsibility for astronomical matters. In later periods, the content of these reports was reviewed and resealed by the Daijōkan or {{ill|Sesshō and Kampaku|ja|摂政・関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sesshō and Kampaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being resubmitted to the Onmyōryō (as described in works such as {{ill|Saigūki|ja|西宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigūki|1=Seikyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627667}}, {{ill|Shingi-shiki|ja|新儀式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingi-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}}). Portions of the reports pertaining to predictions were deemed state secrets and excluded from official records managed by {{ill|Naiki|ja|内記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki|qq=}}.

The documents used for such divinations included the {{ill|Tenmon Yōroku|ja|天文要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Yōroku|1=Q11442665|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442665}}, {{ill|Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Teiran|ja|帝覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiran|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenmonroku|ja|天文録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja|天地瑞祥志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja|指掌宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Senji Ryakketsu|en|Senji Ryakketsu|WD=Q7450796|lt=Senji Ryakketsu|1=Senji Ryakketsu}}, and {{ill|Rekirin|ja|暦林|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekirin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

After the establishment of the {{ill|Kurōdodokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōdodokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}}, Tenmon Hakase bypassed the Onmyōryō and submitted reports directly to the {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo|1=Kurōdo-dokoro}}, who relayed them to the emperor. In urgent cases, such as late at night or early morning, Tenmon Hakase were allowed to report directly to the emperor. The {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}} from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} notes that Tenmon Hakase and qualified secret envoys must immediately report any celestial phenomena to the {{ill|Nairan|en|Nairan|WD=Q5365623|lt=Nairan|1=Nairan}}, or regent, without concern for their attire.

Over time, during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, Tenmon Mitsusō privileges became restricted to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}, both of which had deep connections to {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}} of astrology and astronomy. Notably, during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, even the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, associated with calendar-making, were occasionally granted Tenmon Mitsusō rights.
*

{{nihongo|'''Tenmon Missō'''|天文密奏}} refers to the secret reporting to a ruler of observations of abnormal {{ill|astronomical phenomena|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical phenomena|1=Astronomical events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751989}} and their interpretations (predictions) based on {{ill|Astrology|en|Astrology|WD=Q34362|lt=astrology|1=Astrology}} in ancient {{ill|East Asia|en|East Asia|WD=Q27231|lt=East Asia|1=East Asia}}. It is also known as {{nihongo|'''Tenmon Sō'''|天文奏}}.

== Overview ==
In ancient {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China (region)|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}, the stars in the heavens were thought to symbolize the {{ill|State (polity)|en|State (polity)|WD=Q7275|lt=state|1=State (polity)}} on Earth, and any anomalies in their movements were perceived as portents of political upheaval ({{ill|Mandate of Heaven|ja|上天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandate of Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} thought). Therefore, it was considered crucial for the ruler to promptly grasp such celestial events as a matter of state governance. Furthermore, it was deemed the duty of the {{ill|Son of Heaven|en|Son of Heaven|WD=Q1147432|lt=Son of Heaven|1=Son of Heaven}} to interpret the heavenly will manifest in astronomical phenomena, establish correct calendars and timekeeping, and bestow them upon the people. The {{ill|Book of Jin|en|Book of Jin|WD=Q1154753|lt=Book of Jin|1=Book of Jin}}’s "Astronomical Records," used as a textbook for {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, states that "astronomical anomalies may be omens of fortune, but most are portents of disaster." Thus, it became necessary for those versed in {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=astronomy|1=Astronomy}} and astrology to report such anomalies and their interpretations to the ruler. This practice was termed "Tenmon Mitsusō."

In Japan, when abnormalities were observed, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (including assistant Tenmon Hakase) or individuals granted a Tenmon Mitsusō {{ill|Senji|ja|宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senji|qq=}} were required to interpret the phenomena based on past cases and submit a sealed report, through the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, to the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. The {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code|1=Yōrō Code}}’s {{ill|Zatsuryō|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zatsuryō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} specifies: "If there are minor auspicious or inauspicious events, report them to the Onmyōryō. Afterward, seal them seasonally and send them to the Ministry of the Interior to be archived in the national records." This bypassing of the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|WD=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}}, the highest organ of governance, reflected the emperor's exclusive responsibility for astronomical matters. In later periods, the content of these reports was reviewed and resealed by the Daijōkan or {{ill|Sesshō and Kampaku|ja|摂政・関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sesshō and Kampaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being resubmitted to the Onmyōryō (as described in works such as {{ill|Saigūki|ja|西宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigūki|1=Seikyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627667}}, {{ill|Shingi-shiki|ja|新儀式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingi-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}}). Portions of the reports pertaining to predictions were deemed state secrets and excluded from official records managed by {{ill|Naiki|ja|内記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki|qq=}}.

The documents used for such divinations included the {{ill|Tenmon Yōroku|ja|天文要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Yōroku|1=Q11442665|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442665}}, {{ill|Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Teiran|ja|帝覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiran|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenmonroku|ja|天文録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja|天地瑞祥志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja|指掌宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Senji Ryakketsu|en|Senji Ryakketsu|WD=Q7450796|lt=Senji Ryakketsu|1=Senji Ryakketsu}}, and {{ill|Rekirin|ja|暦林|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekirin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

After the establishment of the {{ill|Kurōdodokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōdodokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}}, Tenmon Hakase bypassed the Onmyōryō and submitted reports directly to the {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo|1=Kurōdo-dokoro}}, who relayed them to the emperor. In urgent cases, such as late at night or early morning, Tenmon Hakase were allowed to report directly to the emperor. The {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}} from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} notes that Tenmon Hakase and qualified secret envoys must immediately report any celestial phenomena to the {{ill|Nairan|en|Nairan|WD=Q5365623|lt=Nairan|1=Nairan}}, or regent, without concern for their attire.

Over time, during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, Tenmon Mitsusō privileges became restricted to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}, both of which had deep connections to {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}} of astrology and astronomy. Notably, during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, even the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, associated with calendar-making, were occasionally granted Tenmon Mitsusō rights.
*

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天文密奏]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天文密奏]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天文密奏]]



 


{{nihongo|'''Tenmon Missō'''|天文密奏}} refers to the secret reporting to a ruler of observations of abnormal {{ill|astronomical phenomena|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical phenomena|1=Astronomical events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751989}} and their interpretations (predictions) based on {{ill|Astrology|en|Astrology|WD=Q34362|lt=astrology|1=Astrology}} in ancient {{ill|East Asia|en|East Asia|WD=Q27231|lt=East Asia|1=East Asia}}. It is also known as {{nihongo|'''Tenmon Sō'''|天文奏}}.

== Overview ==
In ancient {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China (region)|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}, the stars in the heavens were thought to symbolize the {{ill|State (polity)|en|State (polity)|WD=Q7275|lt=state|1=State (polity)}} on Earth, and any anomalies in their movements were perceived as portents of political upheaval ({{ill|Mandate of Heaven|ja|上天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandate of Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} thought). Therefore, it was considered crucial for the ruler to promptly grasp such celestial events as a matter of state governance. Furthermore, it was deemed the duty of the {{ill|Son of Heaven|en|Son of Heaven|WD=Q1147432|lt=Son of Heaven|1=Son of Heaven}} to interpret the heavenly will manifest in astronomical phenomena, establish correct calendars and timekeeping, and bestow them upon the people. The {{ill|Book of Jin|en|Book of Jin|WD=Q1154753|lt=Book of Jin|1=Book of Jin}}’s "Astronomical Records," used as a textbook for {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, states that "astronomical anomalies may be omens of fortune, but most are portents of disaster." Thus, it became necessary for those versed in {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=astronomy|1=Astronomy}} and astrology to report such anomalies and their interpretations to the ruler. This practice was termed "Tenmon Mitsusō."

In Japan, when abnormalities were observed, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (including assistant Tenmon Hakase) or individuals granted a Tenmon Mitsusō {{ill|Senji|ja|宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senji|qq=}} were required to interpret the phenomena based on past cases and submit a sealed report, through the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, to the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. The {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code|1=Yōrō Code}}’s {{ill|Zatsuryō|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zatsuryō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} specifies: "If there are minor auspicious or inauspicious events, report them to the Onmyōryō. Afterward, seal them seasonally and send them to the Ministry of the Interior to be archived in the national records." This bypassing of the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|WD=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}}, the highest organ of governance, reflected the emperor's exclusive responsibility for astronomical matters. In later periods, the content of these reports was reviewed and resealed by the Daijōkan or {{ill|Sesshō and Kampaku|ja|摂政・関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sesshō and Kampaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being resubmitted to the Onmyōryō (as described in works such as {{ill|Saigūki|ja|西宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigūki|1=Seikyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627667}}, {{ill|Shingi-shiki|ja|新儀式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingi-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}}). Portions of the reports pertaining to predictions were deemed state secrets and excluded from official records managed by {{ill|Naiki|ja|内記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki|qq=}}.

The documents used for such divinations included the {{ill|Tenmon Yōroku|ja|天文要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Yōroku|1=Q11442665|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442665}}, {{ill|Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Teiran|ja|帝覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiran|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenmonroku|ja|天文録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja|天地瑞祥志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Zuisenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja|指掌宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishō Sukuyō-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Senji Ryakketsu|en|Senji Ryakketsu|WD=Q7450796|lt=Senji Ryakketsu|1=Senji Ryakketsu}}, and {{ill|Rekirin|ja|暦林|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekirin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

After the establishment of the {{ill|Kurōdodokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōdodokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}}, Tenmon Hakase bypassed the Onmyōryō and submitted reports directly to the {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo|1=Kurōdo-dokoro}}, who relayed them to the emperor. In urgent cases, such as late at night or early morning, Tenmon Hakase were allowed to report directly to the emperor. The {{ill|Kinjishō|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinjishō|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}} from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} notes that Tenmon Hakase and qualified secret envoys must immediately report any celestial phenomena to the {{ill|Nairan|en|Nairan|WD=Q5365623|lt=Nairan|1=Nairan}}, or regent, without concern for their attire.

Over time, during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, Tenmon Mitsusō privileges became restricted to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}, both of which had deep connections to {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}} of astrology and astronomy. Notably, during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, even the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, associated with calendar-making, were occasionally granted Tenmon Mitsusō rights.
*
